; pr24p — Pascal Runtime Library
; Phase 0: Hand-written .spc stubs for p-code VM syscall wrappers

; _p24p_write_int ( n -- )
; Print signed integer to UART as decimal.
; Handles negative numbers (prints '-' prefix).
; Uses div/mod by 10 to extract digits, pushes onto eval stack
; in reverse order with a zero sentinel, then prints from most
; significant to least significant via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_int 1
    enter 1
    loada 0              ; load argument n
    ; check for negative
    dup                  ; n n
    push 0               ; n n 0
    lt                   ; n (n<0?)
    jz positive
    ; print minus sign
    push 45              ; n '-'
    sys 1                ; n
    neg                  ; -n
positive:
    storel 0             ; local0 = abs(n)
    push 0               ; push sentinel
extract:
    loadl 0              ; load n
    push 10
    mod                  ; n % 10
    push 48
    add                  ; n % 10 + '0' = digit char
    loadl 0
    push 10
    div                  ; n / 10
    storel 0             ; update n = n / 10
    loadl 0
    jnz extract          ; if n != 0, extract more digits
print:
    dup
    jz done
    sys 1                ; PUTC digit
    jmp print
done:
    drop                 ; discard sentinel
    leave
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_bool ( b -- )
; Print TRUE or FALSE to UART.
; Compares argument to 0: if zero prints FALSE, otherwise prints TRUE.
; Each character is pushed and output via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_bool 1
    enter 0
    loada 0              ; load argument b
    jz print_false
    ; print "TRUE"
    push 84              ; 'T'
    sys 1
    push 82              ; 'R'
    sys 1
    push 85              ; 'U'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
    jmp bool_done
print_false:
    ; print "FALSE"
    push 70              ; 'F'
    sys 1
    push 65              ; 'A'
    sys 1
    push 76              ; 'L'
    sys 1
    push 83              ; 'S'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
bool_done:
    leave
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_ln ( -- )
; Print newline (LF) to UART via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_ln 0
    enter 0
    push 10              ; LF
    sys 1
    leave
    ret 0
.end

; pvmasm.s — P-Code Assembler + VM for COR24

; Integrated assembler and virtual machine:

;   2. Two-pass assembly to bytecode (code+data contiguous in code_buf)
;   3. Execute assembled bytecode in the p-code VM
;
; Assembly phases:
;   Pass 1: collect symbols (labels, procs, consts, globals, data), compute sizes
;   Pass 2: emit bytecode, resolve symbol references
;
; Token types:

;   1 = TOK_NL      newline
;   2 = TOK_NUM     integer literal (value in tok_value)
;   3 = TOK_IDENT   identifier/mnemonic/name (string in tok_buf)
;   4 = TOK_DIR     directive without '.' (string in tok_buf)

; read_name — read alphanumeric/underscore chars into tok_buf
; ============================================================
; Precondition: lex_char is first char of name (alpha or underscore)
; Postcondition: tok_buf filled and null-terminated, tok_len set
; Non-leaf. Clobbers: r0, r1, r2, fp.
read_name:
    push r1
    ; Initialize write pointer
    la r0, tok_buf
    la r2, tok_ptr
    sw r0, 0(r2)

rn_loop:
    la r2, lex_char
    lbu r0, 0(r2)

    ; Check underscore (95)
    lc r2, 95
    ceq r0, r2
    brt rn_store

    ; Check digit: 48 <= r0 <= 57
    lc r2, 48
    clu r0, r2
    brt rn_not_digit
    lc r2, 58
    clu r0, r2
    brt rn_store
rn_not_digit:

    ; Check uppercase: 65 <= r0 <= 90
    lc r2, 65
    clu r0, r2
    brt rn_done
    lc r2, 91
    clu r0, r2
    brt rn_store

    ; Check lowercase: 97 <= r0 <= 122
    lc r2, 97
    clu r0, r2
    brt rn_done
    lc r2, 123
    clu r0, r2
    brf rn_done
    ; Fall through: is lowercase letter

rn_store:
    ; Store char at tok_ptr, advance ptr
    la r2, tok_ptr
    push r2
    pop fp
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 1
    sw r2, 0(fp)
    ; Advance lexer
    la r2, lex_advance
    jal r1, (r2)
    la r0, rn_loop
    jmp (r0)

rn_done:
    ; Null-terminate tok_buf
    la r2, tok_ptr
    lw r2, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    ; Calculate length: end - tok_buf
    la r0, tok_buf

file.content:
; sym_find — search symbol table for name in tok_buf
; Returns value in r0. Prints error if not found, returns 0.
; Non-leaf. Clobbers: r0, r1, r2.
sym_find:
    push r1
    ; Check if table is empty
    la r2, sym_count
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ceq r0, z
    brf sf_start
    la r0, sf_not_found
    jmp (r0)
sf_start:
    ; Init search state
    la r2, sym_count
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    la r2, sf_count
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    lc r0, 0
    la r2, sf_index
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, sym_table
    la r2, sf_ptr
    sw r0, 0(r2)

sf_loop:
    ; Check index < count
    la r2, sf_index
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    push r0
    la r2, sf_count
    lw r2, 0(r2)
    pop r0
    clu r0, r2
    brf sf_not_found

    ; Get name address: name_pool + entry[0]
    la r2, sf_ptr
    lw r2, 0(r2)
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    la r2, name_pool
    add r0, r2

    ; Compare with tok_buf
    la r2, str_eq_a
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, tok_buf
    la r2, str_eq_b
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    la r2, str_eq
    jal r1, (r2)
    ; r0 = 1 if match
    ceq r0, z
    brt sf_next

    ; Found! Return value (word 2 at offset 6)
    la r2, sf_ptr
    lw r2, 0(r2)
    lw r0, 6(r2)
    pop r1
    jmp (r1)

sf_next:
    ; Advance pointer by 9
    la r2, sf_ptr
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r0, 9
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    ; Increment index
    la r2, sf_index
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, sf_loop
    jmp (r0)

sf_not_found:
    la r0, msg_err_sym
    la r2, uart_puts
    jal r1, (r2)
    la r0, tok_buf
    la r2, uart_puts
    jal r1, (r2)
    lc r0, 10
    la r2, uart_putc
    jal r1, (r2)
    lc r0, 0
    pop r1
    jmp (r1)

; ============================================================
; Symbol table — copy name to name pool
; ============================================================

; sym_name_copy — copy tok_buf into name_pool, return offset in r0
; Non-leaf. Clobbers: r0, r1, r2.
sym_name_copy:
    push r1
    ; Calculate offset: name_pool_ptr - name_pool
    la r2, name_pool_ptr

; pr24p — Pascal Runtime Library
; Phase 0: Hand-written .spc stubs for p-code VM syscall wrappers

; _p24p_write_int ( n -- )
; Print signed integer to UART as decimal.
; Handles negative numbers (prints '-' prefix).
; Uses div/mod by 10 to extract digits, pushes onto eval stack
; in reverse order with a zero sentinel, then prints from most
; significant to least significant via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_int 1
    enter 1
    loada 0              ; load argument n
    ; check for negative
    dup                  ; n n
    push 0               ; n n 0
    lt                   ; n (n<0?)
    jz positive
    ; print minus sign
    push 45              ; n '-'
    sys 1                ; n
    neg                  ; -n
positive:
    storel 0             ; local0 = abs(n)
    push 0               ; push sentinel
extract:
    loadl 0              ; load n
    push 10
    mod                  ; n % 10
    push 48
    add                  ; n % 10 + '0' = digit char
    loadl 0
    push 10
    div                  ; n / 10
    storel 0             ; update n = n / 10
    loadl 0
    jnz extract          ; if n != 0, extract more digits
print:
    dup
    jz done
    sys 1                ; PUTC digit
    jmp print
done:
    drop                 ; discard sentinel
    leave
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_bool ( b -- )
; Print TRUE or FALSE to UART.
; Compares argument to 0: if zero prints FALSE, otherwise prints TRUE.
; Each character is pushed and output via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_bool 1
    enter 1
    loada 0              ; load argument b
    jz wb_false
    ; print "TRUE"
    push 84              ; 'T'
    sys 1
    push 82              ; 'R'
    sys 1
    push 85              ; 'U'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
    jmp wb_done
wb_false:
    ; print "FALSE"
    push 70              ; 'F'
    sys 1
    push 65              ; 'A'
    sys 1
    push 76              ; 'L'
    sys 1
    push 83              ; 'S'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
wb_done:
    leave
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_ln ( -- )
; Print newline (LF) to UART via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_ln 0
    enter 1
    push 10              ; LF
    sys 1
    leave
    ret 0
.end

; pr24p — Pascal Runtime Library
; Phase 0: Hand-written .spc stubs for p-code VM syscall wrappers

; _p24p_write_int ( n -- )
; Print signed integer to UART as decimal.
; Handles negative numbers (prints '-' prefix).
; Uses div/mod by 10 to extract digits, pushes onto eval stack
; in reverse order with a zero sentinel, then prints from most
; significant to least significant via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_int 1
    enter 1
    loada 0              ; load argument n
    ; check for negative
    dup                  ; n n
    push 0               ; n n 0
    lt                   ; n (n<0?)
    jz positive
    ; print minus sign
    push 45              ; n '-'
    sys 1                ; n
    neg                  ; -n
positive:
    storel 0             ; local0 = abs(n)
    push 0               ; push sentinel
extract:
    loadl 0              ; load n
    push 10
    mod                  ; n % 10
    push 48
    add                  ; n % 10 + '0' = digit char
    loadl 0
    push 10
    div                  ; n / 10
    storel 0             ; update n = n / 10
    loadl 0
    jnz extract          ; if n != 0, extract more digits
print:
    dup
    jz done
    sys 1                ; PUTC digit
    jmp print
done:
    drop                 ; discard sentinel
    leave
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_bool ( b -- )
; Print TRUE or FALSE to UART.
; Compares argument to 0: if zero prints FALSE, otherwise prints TRUE.
; Each character is pushed and output via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_bool 1
    enter 1
    loada 0              ; load argument b
    jz wb_false
    ; print "TRUE"
    push 84              ; 'T'
    sys 1
    push 82              ; 'R'
    sys 1
    push 85              ; 'U'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
    jmp wb_done
wb_false:
    ; print "FALSE"
    push 70              ; 'F'
    sys 1
    push 65              ; 'A'
    sys 1
    push 76              ; 'L'
    sys 1
    push 83              ; 'S'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
wb_done:
    leave
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_ln ( -- )
; Print newline (LF) to UART via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_ln 0
    enter 1
    push 10              ; LF
    sys 1
    leave
    ret 0
.end

content.text:
So `.end` emits a `leave` opcode (65). Now it makes sense — the `.proc` emits `enter` and `.end` emits `leave`. Looking at hello.spc: no explicit `enter`/`leave` because `.proc`/`.end` handle them.

Now looking at my decoded bytecodes: position 65 was `drop` (4), position 66-67 was `ret 1` (52, 1). So write_int's `ret 1` is at position 67. Then:

Position 68: leave (65) — from `.end` of write_int

This is wrong! The `ret 1` at position 66-67 already returns. Then position 68 is an unreachable `leave` from `.end`. But position 68 is also where write_bool starts.

Wait — `.end` emits `leave` AFTER the `ret`. So the code is: `drop, ret 1, leave`. The `ret 1` returns before reaching `leave`. Then write_bool proc starts after that `leave` byte.

Position 68: 65 → leave (from .end of write_int)
Position 69-70: 64 0 → enter 0 (from .proc _p24p_write_bool 0)
Position 71-72: 72 0 → loada 0

But `call _p24p_write_bool` would jump to... what address? The proc was registered in pass 1 with `code_addr` at that point. Let me think about the symbol registration.

In pass 1, when `.proc _p24p_write_bool 0` is processed:
- The current `code_addr` is saved as the proc's value
- Then `code_addr += 2` (for enter opcode + nlocals byte)

So the proc address points to the `enter` instruction, which is position 69. That means `call _p24p_write_bool` jumps to position 69 (the enter). Good.

Now the `leave` at position 68 (from `.end` of write_int) is dead code — never reached. But it still takes 1 byte. OK.

Now let me continue decoding write_bool:
Position 69-70: 64 0 → enter 0
Position 71-72: 72 0 → loada 0
Position 73-76: 49 105 0 0 → jz 105 (wb_f should be at addr 105)

But wait — the `ERR: wb_f` message means the symbol `wb_f` wasn't found. But it resolved to some address (the 105 in the jz). Actually, when the symbol is not found, sym_find returns 0. But the output shows 105. Hmm.

Actually wait, maybe the ERR happened during pass 1, not pass 2. Or maybe the error is a warning and it still emits bytecodes. But sym_find returns 0 when not found... so jz target should be 0, not 105.

; ============================================================
; Parser — instruction handler
; ============================================================

; handle_instr — look up mnemonic, emit or count instruction
; Non-leaf. Clobbers: r0, r1, r2.
handle_instr:
    push r1
    ; Look up mnemonic
    la r2, mnem_lookup
    jal r1, (r2)
    ; r0 = 1 if found, 0 if not
    ceq r0, z
    brf hi_found
    ; Unknown mnemonic
    la r0, msg_err_mnem
    la r2, uart_puts
    jal r1, (r2)
    la r0, tok_buf
    la r2, uart_puts
    jal r1, (r2)
    lc r0, 10
    la r2, uart_putc
    jal r1, (r2)
    la r2, skip_to_eol
    jal r1, (r2)
    pop r1
    jmp (r1)

hi_found:
    ; cur_opcode and cur_optype are set
    ; Check pass number
    la r2, pass_num
    lbu r0, 0(r2)
    lc r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2
    brf hi_do_pass2
    ; ---- Pass 1: count instruction size ----
    la r0, hi_pass1
    jmp (r0)
hi_do_pass2:
    ; ---- Pass 2: emit opcode byte first ----
    la r2, cur_opcode
    lbu r0, 0(r2)
    la r2, emit_byte
    jal r1, (r2)
    la r0, hi_p2_operand
    jmp (r0)

; ---- Pass 1: add instruction size to code_addr ----
hi_pass1:

    la r2, code_addr

    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    ; Now add operand size based on type
    la r2, cur_optype
    lbu r0, 0(r2)

    la r0, hi_p1_done
    jmp (r0)
hi_p1_not_t1:

    add r0, 4

    jmp (r0)

    ; type 4 (D8_O8): +2
    la r2, code_addr
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    add r0, 2
    sw r0, 0(r2)

hi_p1_done:
    ; Skip remaining tokens on line
    la r2, skip_to_eol

; resolve_operand — resolve current token to a value
; If NUM, returns tok_value. If IDENT, looks up symbol.
; Returns value in r0.
; Non-leaf. Clobbers: r0, r1, r2.
resolve_operand:
    push r1
    la r2, tok_type
    lbu r0, 0(r2)
    ; NUM?
    lc r2, 2
    ceq r0, r2
    brf ro_not_num
    la r2, tok_value
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    pop r1
    jmp (r1)
ro_not_num:
    ; IDENT?
    lc r2, 3
    ceq r0, r2
    brf ro_default
    la r2, sym_find
    jal r1, (r2)
    pop r1
    jmp (r1)
ro_default:
    lc r0, 0
    pop r1
    jmp (r1)

; ============================================================
; patch_symbols — fix data/global offsets after pass 1
; ============================================================

; patch_symbols — add code_size to SYM_DATA, code_size+data_size to SYM_GLOBAL
; Non-leaf. Clobbers: r0, r1, r2.
patch_symbols:
    push r1
    la r2, sym_count
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ceq r0, z
    brf ps_start
    pop r1
    jmp (r1)
ps_start:
    la r2, sym_count
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    la r2, ps_count
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, sym_table

hello.spc works — prints "Hello\n". So loada works correctly in hello.spc. The difference must be something specific to my code.

    push 48
    add

    ret 1

content.text:

'CODE 64 0 1 1 0 0 51 113 0 0 51 182 0 0 1 0 0 0 51 113 0 0 51 182 0 0 1 42 0 0 51 113 0 0 51 182 0 0 51 182 0 0 0 65 [UART TX @ 26310000] '
'[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    storel 0             ; local0 = abs(n)[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    push 0               ; push sentinel[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'extract:[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    loadl 0              ; load n[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    push 10[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    mod                  ; n % 10[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    push 48[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    add                  ; n % 10 + '0' = digit char[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    loadl 0[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    push 10[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    div                  ; n / 10[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    storel 0             ; update n = n / 10[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    loadl 0[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    jnz extract          ; if n != 0, extract more digits[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'wi_print:[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    dup[UART TX @ 26310000] '
'    jz wi_done[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    sys 1                ; PUTC digit[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    jmp wi_print[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'wi_done:[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    drop                 ; discard sentinel[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    ret 1[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'.end[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'; _p24p_write_bool ( b -- )[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'; Print TRUE or FALSE to UART.[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'; Compares argument to 0: if zero prints FALSE, otherwise prints TRUE.[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'; Each character is pushed and output via sys 1 (PUTC).[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'.proc _p24p_write_bool 0[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    loada 0              ; load argument b[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    jz wb_false[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    ; print "TRUE"[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    push 84              ; 'T'[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    sys 1[UART TX @ 26320000] '
'    push 82              ; 'R'[UART TX @ 26320000] '

     4→; _p24p_write_int ( n -- )
     5→; Print signed integer to UART as decimal.
     6→; Handles negative numbers (prints '-' prefix).
     7→; Uses div/mod by 10 to extract digits, pushes onto eval stack
     8→; in reverse order with a zero sentinel, then prints from most
     9→; significant to least significant via sys 1 (PUTC).
    10→.proc _p24p_write_int 1
    11→    loada 0              ; load argument n

    14→    push 0               ; n n 0
    15→    lt                   ; n (n<0?)
    16→    jz positive

    18→    push 45              ; n '-'
    19→    sys 1                ; n
    20→    neg                  ; -n

file.content:
; pr24p — Pascal Runtime Library
; Phase 0: Hand-written .spc stubs for p-code VM syscall wrappers

; _p24p_write_int ( n -- )
; Print signed integer to UART as decimal.
; Handles negative numbers (prints '-' prefix).
; Uses div/mod by 10 to extract digits, pushes onto eval stack
; in reverse order with a zero sentinel, then prints from most
; significant to least significant via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_int 1
    loada 0              ; load argument n
    ; check for negative
    dup                  ; n n
    push 0               ; n n 0
    lt                   ; n (n<0?)
    jz positive
    ; print minus sign
    push 45              ; n '-'
    sys 1                ; n
    neg                  ; -n
positive:
    storel 0             ; local0 = abs(n)
    push 0               ; push sentinel
extract:
    loadl 0              ; load n
    push 10
    mod                  ; n % 10
    push 48
    add                  ; n % 10 + '0' = digit char
    loadl 0
    push 10
    div                  ; n / 10
    storel 0             ; update n = n / 10
    loadl 0
    jnz extract          ; if n != 0, extract more digits
wi_print:
    dup
    jz wi_done
    sys 1                ; PUTC digit
    jmp wi_print
wi_done:
    drop                 ; discard sentinel
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_bool ( b -- )
; Print TRUE or FALSE to UART.
; Compares argument to 0: if zero prints FALSE, otherwise prints TRUE.
; Each character is pushed and output via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_bool 0
    loada 0              ; load argument b
    jz wb_false
    ; print "TRUE"
    push 84              ; 'T'
    sys 1
    push 82              ; 'R'
    sys 1
    push 85              ; 'U'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
    jmp wb_done
wb_false:
    ; print "FALSE"
    push 70              ; 'F'
    sys 1
    push 65              ; 'A'
    sys 1
    push 76              ; 'L'
    sys 1
    push 83              ; 'S'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
wb_done:
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_ln ( -- )
; Print newline (LF) to UART via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_ln 0
    push 10              ; LF
    sys 1
    ret 0
.end
